title: Do not default to five bottom tabs
summary: AI-generated mobile navigation universally defaults to a five-tab bottom bar with icon+label and a hamburger menu, regardless of the app's actual navigation architecture.
instruction: Choose mobile navigation based on the app's information architecture, not the five-tab template. Consider gesture-based navigation, top tabs, context-revealing navigation, or fewer bottom tabs. Reserve the five-tab bar for apps with five genuine primary destinations.
rationale: The five-tab bottom bar is a default pattern applied to apps regardless of architecture. It forces fit for apps with 3 or fewer primary destinations, creates a 'More' dumping ground, and competes with platform gesture navigation on modern phones.
violations: Exactly five bottom tabs with icon + label on every screen
Tabs always include Home, Search, Activity/Notifications, Profile, and More/Settings
Active tab uses brand primary color fill on outlined icons
Hamburger menu in top-left corner coexisting with bottom tabs
Same tab structure regardless of app purpose (productivity, social, utility)
controls: Three- or four-tab bar matching the actual number of primary destinations
Gesture-driven navigation without persistent bottom bar
Top tab navigation for content-category apps
Single-screen app where navigation is embedded in the content
borderlineCases: Content-consumption apps with five genuine primary destinations
contextualExceptions: Platform design guidelines that recommend bottom tabs (iOS/Android conventions)
detectorSignal: A persistent bottom bar containing 5 evenly-spaced icon+label items, where one uses a filled icon style.
matcherProposal: Detect a bottom-positioned navigation bar with 5 child items containing both an icon and a text label.
detectorLimitations: Cannot distinguish essential 5-tab architectures from defaulted ones
falsePositiveScenarios: Apps with five genuine navigation destinations
mitigationStrategy: Flag when the app structure suggests fewer primary destinations would be more appropriate.

fixturePlans.violation: Mobile app with 5 bottom tabs (Home, Search, Activity, Profile, More), hamburger menu, filled active tab icon.
fixturePlans.control: App with 3 bottom tabs matching 3 primary destinations, no hamburger menu.
fixturePlans.borderline: Social app with 5 genuine primary sections matching the five-tab pattern.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
